description: Survival probability decay parameters per condition. Formula: survival_pct = base_survival * exp(-decay_rate * max(0, delay_minutes - grace_period_minutes))

cardiac_arrest.notes: Brain damage begins at 4 min without CPR. Survival drops ~10% per minute after.

STEMI.notes: Door-to-balloon target is 90 min. Every 30 min delay increases mortality by ~7.5%.

acute_stroke.notes: tPA window is 4.5 hours. 1.9 million neurons lost per minute without treatment.

severe_trauma.notes: Classic golden hour. Hemorrhagic shock is primary killer.

respiratory_failure.notes: Without ventilatory support, hypoxic damage accelerates rapidly.

chest_pain_unstable_angina.notes: Can progress to STEMI. Needs monitoring and cath lab access.

moderate_trauma.notes: Internal bleeding risk. CT scan needed to rule out organ damage.

seizure_episode.notes: Status epilepticus risk if prolonged. Need benzodiazepines and monitoring.

allergic_anaphylaxis.notes: Epinephrine needed within minutes. Airway compromise is main risk.

spinal_injury.notes: Immobilization critical. Secondary injury from swelling peaks at 2 hours.

minor_fracture.notes: Low urgency. Pain management primary concern.

abdominal_emergency.notes: Perforation or hemorrhage risk. Surgical intervention may be needed.

diabetic_crisis.notes: DKA or hypoglycemia. IV access and glucose/insulin management critical.

drowning.notes: Hypoxic brain injury begins within minutes. Aggressive airway management needed.

formula: survival_pct = base_survival * exp(-decay_rate * max(0, delay_minutes - grace_period_minutes))
